Our goal is to isolate the variable `b`.

To decide whether an equation is  

• conditional (true for one or more specific numbers),  
• an identity (true for every real number), or  
• a contradiction (never true),

solve the equation and see what happens.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
1) 9u + 32 = 15(u – 4)

Distribute and collect like terms  

 9u + 32 = 15u – 60  
 9u + 32 – 15u = –60  
 –6u + 32 = –60  
 –6u = –92  
 u = 92/6 = 46/3

The equation is satisfied only when u = 46/3, so it is a
conditional equation.

Solution set: {46/3}

--------------------------------------------------------------------
2) –3(2u + 21) = –6(u + 7)      (the right-hand member is required to make a true “equation”)

Distribute both sides  

 –6u – 63 = –6u – 42

Add 6u to both sides  

 –63 = –42

This statement is false, so no value of u can satisfy the equation.
Hence it is a contradiction.

Solution set: ∅ (no solution)

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Summary  
• 9u + 32 = 15(u – 4) → conditional; u = 46/3  
• –3(2u + 21) = –6(u + 7) → contradiction; no solution

Question: Solve the formula $$V = L W H$$ for $$H$$ .
o3 Answer:
The given formula is  

        V = L · W · H  

(where V is volume, L is length, W is width, H is height).  

To solve for H, isolate H on one side of the equation.

1. Divide both sides by L:  
   V / L = W · H

2. Now divide both sides by W:  
   (V / L) / W = H

3. Combine the two denominators:  
   H = V / (L · W)

Thus,

        H = V / (L W)
